title: Stop discarding herdr's error code, and give the three codes callers act on their own variants
action: THE DEFECT: herdr answers a refused request with {code, message} -- both fields always present (upstreams/herdr/src/api/schema/response.rs:37-40) -- and parse_response (src/herdr/socket.rs:205-221) reads ONLY message, throwing the code away. Every server refusal, whatever it was, collapses into HerdrError::Request(String). Slice 2's create verbs cannot exist on top of that: an auto-generated agent name that collides must be retried silently, and you cannot retry what you cannot recognise. WHAT TO ADD to HerdrError in src/herdr/mod.rs (the enum is at :17-29): AgentNameTaken { name: String }, WorkspaceNotFound { workspace_id: String }, InvalidAgentArgv(String), and Remote { code: String, message: String }. Those three typed variants are not an arbitrary selection -- they are exactly the codes a caller BRANCHES on (decision cbb74712): agent_name_taken drives the retry loop in cell new-shell-new-agent-5, workspace_not_found tells slice 4 the phone picked a destination that has since vanished, invalid_agent_argv lets slice 3 reject a malformed preset at config-load time. Every OTHER upstream code -- invalid_agent_name, agent_placement_not_found, agent_placement_conflict, agent_start_failed, tab_create_failed, invalid_env -- has exactly one caller behaviour, show the message, and goes to Remote with its code string intact. Do NOT add a variant per code. WHAT parse_response MUST DO: read error.code alongside error.message; map the three known codes to their typed variants; map everything else to Remote{code, message}. THE BOUNDARY THAT MATTERS: HerdrError::Request keeps its current meaning and its current construction sites -- WE failed to make the request (serialize, connect, write, read). Remote means the SERVER answered and refused. Do not repurpose Request, do not touch the io-error mapping at socket.rs:161-200, and do not touch unavailable_connect_error (socket.rs:123-135). MISSING CODE IS NOT MALFORMED: an error envelope with a message but no code maps to Remote with an empty code string and the message intact. Mapping it to Malformed would replace the server's own explanation with 'malformed herdr response' and hide the only thing the operator could act on. AgentNameTaken's name and WorkspaceNotFound's workspace_id: herdr's message text embeds them but parsing them out of prose is brittle -- carry what the CALLER passed in, by having the create methods in later cells attach it, and in parse_response construct these variants with the empty string. Say so in a comment so the next reader does not think it is a bug. ONE EXISTING TEST CHANGES: parse_response_maps_error (src/herdr/socket.rs, in the tests module ~:367-535) asserts today's message-only collapse into Request. That assertion is the defect being fixed, so update it deliberately -- it is the ONLY existing proof this cell is permitted to change. Every other test in the repo must stay green untouched. Do NOT add tab.create or agent.start here; this cell is the error surface only. TEST NAMING IS PART OF THE CONTRACT: every test you add must be prefixed errcode_ (for example errcode_agent_name_taken_maps_to_typed_variant, errcode_unknown_code_preserved_in_remote, errcode_missing_code_is_remote_not_malformed, errcode_local_io_failure_still_maps_to_request). The verify counts passing errcode_ tests and requires at least 5, so it fails on an untouched tree -- a green verify is evidence the work exists, not evidence the suite was already green.

truths: An error envelope with code agent_name_taken produces HerdrError::AgentNameTaken
An error envelope with code workspace_not_found produces HerdrError::WorkspaceNotFound
An error envelope with code invalid_agent_argv produces HerdrError::InvalidAgentArgv
An error envelope with any other code produces HerdrError::Remote carrying that exact code string and the message
An error envelope with a message but no code produces HerdrError::Remote with an empty code and the message intact -- never Malformed
A serialize/connect/write/read failure still produces HerdrError::Request, unchanged
A well-formed result envelope still parses to Ok exactly as before
artifacts: The four new variants on HerdrError in src/herdr/mod.rs, each with a thiserror #[error] message a human can act on
parse_response in src/herdr/socket.rs reading error.code and dispatching to the typed variants or Remote
At least 5 tests prefixed errcode_, all passing
The updated parse_response_maps_error assertion, with the old expectation replaced rather than deleted
key_links: upstreams/herdr/src/api/schema/response.rs:37-40 -- ErrorBody is {code: String, message: String}, code is a string not a number, and there is no data field
upstreams/herdr/src/app/agents.rs:208-253 -- agent_start_error_body, the verbatim code strings
decision cbb74712 -- why three typed variants and not one per code
prohibitions: Do not add a HerdrError variant per upstream error code
Do not repurpose or widen HerdrError::Request -- it means WE failed to make the request
Do not map a coded refusal to Malformed under any circumstance
Do not map an error envelope with no code to Malformed -- Remote with an empty code
Do not touch unavailable_connect_error or the io-error mapping inside call()
Do not add tab.create, agent.start, or any trait method -- later cells
Do not change any test other than parse_response_maps_error
Do not parse the name or workspace_id out of herdr's human-readable message text

verify: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- errcode_ 2>&1 | grep -qE 'test result: ok\. ([5-9]|[1-9][0-9]+) passed'

trace.outcome: HerdrError gained AgentNameTaken{name,message}, WorkspaceNotFound{workspace_id,message}, InvalidAgentArgv and Remote{code,message}; parse_response now reads error.code and dispatches to them instead of collapsing every server refusal into Request. Request keeps its meaning. A coded refusal never maps to Malformed, and a refusal with no code maps to Remote with an empty code so the server's message still reaches the operator. 7 errcode_ tests.
trace.verify_output: ORCHESTRATOR'S OWN FRESH RUN, not the worker's report. cargo test --quiet: 180 passed, 0 failed (was 173 before this cell). fmt --all --check: silent. clippy --all-targets -D warnings: silent. errcode_-only run: test result: ok. 7 passed; 0 failed; 173 filtered out. Guard grep exit code: 0. Prior red baseline on the untouched tree recorded on this same cell (0 errcode_ tests, guard exit 1), and an intermediate red where the worker's socket test deadlocked the suite -- both retained in the ledger.
trace.verify_command: cargo test --quiet && cargo fmt --all --check && cargo clippy --all-targets -- -D warnings && cargo test --lib -- errcode_ | grep -qE 'test result ok 5 or more passed'
